content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

September 27, 1976

A-96-258

Mr. Arthur Harris
Benner and Harris
2140 Shattuck Avenue, #1006
Citrus Heights, California 95610

Dear Mr. Harris:

     Thank you for your letter of August 26 in which you inquired about the status of joint powers agencies in their requirement to submit a Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act of 1974.

     The Commission has been in the process of revising its regulations and developing materials to provide agencies required to submit Conflict of Interest Codes additional assistance and guidance in Code preparation. Deadline dates for submission of Codes which were previously established for agencies for which the Fair Political Practices Commission was the "code reviewing body" were suspended in July and reestablished in August -- thus, setting our Code reviewing calendar back several months to allow agencies the opportunity to utilize the Commission's new materials and structure their codes to standards established by the Commission's model Codes, regulation changes, statements of policy and memoranda to Code drafters mailed to all such agencies this month. (See materials enclosed.)

     It is anticipated that deadline dates for joint powers agencies for which the Commission is the code reviewing body will not be established before February, 1977. You will be notified as soon as a deadline date is established for the Metropolitan Transportation Commission. It is not clear from your previous correspondence whether the Association of Bay Area Governments is an agency which must develop a Conflict of Interest Code pursuant to the Act.

Mr. Arthur Harris
September 27, 1976                                Page Two


     Thank you for your interest in the Political Reform
Act.  If we can be of further assistance, please do not
hesitate to call.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division


DLS:glb

Enclosure

LAW OFFICES
BENNER AND HARRIS
FREDERIC C. BENNER
ARTHUR HARRIS
2140 SHATTUCK AVENUE, #1006
BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94704
August 26, 1976

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attention: Marilyn Aguirre

Dear Ms. Aguirre:

As you know from previous correspondence, I am attorney for
the Association of Bay Area Governments and the Metropolitan
Transportation Commission. I have been corresponding with
your Commission since March to determine if these two agencies
are considered agencies for which conflict of interest codes
must be developed and submitted, and if so what would the
deadline dates be for them.

You did advise by telephone call of June 1, 1976 that there
would be no determination concerning these agencies until after
August of 1976 and that I would subsequently be advised of
such determination and if required submission dates would then
be determined.

Since we are approaching the end of August I thought I should
flag this matter to your attention again. Further, recently
because Solano County is a member county of the Metropolitan
Transportation Commission I did receive a copy of a memorandum
prepared by their County Counsel advising that initial submission
dates for draft codes had been suspended and new ones established
which would appear to require adoption of codes in December,
1976 and January, 1977. Finally, I have heard there have been
some revisions as to guidelines suggested in the preparation of
these codes.

At your convenience, I would appreciate a response relating to
each agency, and if it is determined that these codes should
be submitted that reasonable time be allowed for preparation.
If there have been revised guidelines, I would appreciate receiving a copy. Thank you for your courtesy.

Sincerely yours,

AH/mc
ARTHUR HARRIS

sections.question: Mr. Harris inquired about the status of joint powers agencies in their requirement to submit a Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act of 1974, specifically regarding the Association of Bay Area Governments and the Metropolitan Transportation Commission, including applicable deadline dates and any revised guidelines for code preparation.
sections.conclusion: The Commission has suspended and reestablished deadline dates for agencies required to submit Conflict of Interest Codes. It is anticipated that deadline dates for joint powers agencies for which the Commission is the code reviewing body will not be established before February 1977. The Metropolitan Transportation Commission will be notified once a deadline date is established. It is not clear from the correspondence whether the Association of Bay Area Governments is an agency which must develop a Conflict of Interest Code pursuant to the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Are joint powers agencies such as the Metropolitan Transportation Commission and Association of Bay Area Governments required to submit Conflict of Interest Codes under the Political Reform Act of 1974, and what are the applicable deadline dates and current guidelines?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has suspended previous deadline dates and anticipates that new deadline dates for joint powers agencies will not be established before February 1977, with the Metropolitan Transportation Commission to be notified separately. The status of the Association of Bay Area Governments as a required code-submitting agency remains unclear.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (dated August 25, 1976) and FPPC response letter (dated September 27, 1976). Response letter has clear conclusion but no formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers. Confidence reduced due to OCR quality issues and some ambiguity regarding Association of Bay Area Governments status.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Mr. Harris inquired about the status of joint powers agencies in their requirement to submit a Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act of 1974, specifically regarding the Association of Bay Area Governments and the Metropolitan Transportation Commission, including applicable deadline dates and any revised guidelines for code preparation.

CONCLUSION: The Commission has suspended and reestablished deadline dates for agencies required to submit Conflict of Interest Codes. It is anticipated that deadline dates for joint powers agencies for which the Commission is the code reviewing body will not be established before February 1977. The Metropolitan Transportation Commission will be notified once a deadline date is established. It is not clear from the correspondence whether the Association of Bay Area Governments is an agency which must develop a Conflict of Interest Code pursuant to the Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to attorney Arthur Harris regarding the Conflict of Interest Code submission requirements for joint powers agencies, explaining that deadline dates have been suspended and reset, with new guidelines and model codes being provided to agencies, and that new deadline dates for affected agencies will not be established before February 1977.
